steps.title: Find both solutions
steps.content:
An absolute value equation $|x-9|=12$ means the expression inside can be $12$ or $-12$.
$$x-9=12\quad \text{or}\quad x-9=-12$$
$$x=21\quad \text{or}\quad x=-3$$

steps.title: Add the solutions
steps.content: The question asks for the sum of the two solutions. $$21+(-3)=18$$ The answer is 18.
